expectations.text: Chooses a hook (not a skill) because the user wants automatic enforcement
expectations.evidence: Response is entirely hook-based; explains enforcement is automatic and cannot rely on user invoking a skill

expectations.text: Chooses PreToolUse event (not PostToolUse) because it must block before execution
expectations.evidence: Explicit section 'Event: PreToolUse (not PostToolUse)' with reasoning: 'PostToolUse only observes after the fact — the push would have already happened'

expectations.text: Provides a shell script that checks git push commands for main/master patterns
expectations.evidence: block-main-push.sh written; regex `git push\b.*\b(main|master)\b` with examples of what matches/doesn't

expectations.text: Uses non-zero exit (exit 1 or exit 2) to block the tool call
expectations.evidence: Uses exit 2 with message 'BLOCKED: Direct push to main/master is not allowed'

expectations.text: Includes exit 0 for non-matching commands to avoid accidental blocking
expectations.evidence: Section 'Safety: Exit 0 on All Non-Matching Paths' explicitly covers tool != Bash and command != push pattern
settings-snippet.json written; response shows jq merge command for registration
